description: Add image-aware AI analysis, multi-image attachments, mobile-resolution preview, and requirement tree preview for game UI PRD generation.

userStories.title: Support multiple image attachments in the input flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to attach one or more images so that I can provide game UI screenshots, reference images, or assets for the generated interface.
acceptanceCriteria: The attachment control accepts image files
The attachment control supports selecting multiple files in one action
Users can add more than one attachment before generating or previewing output
Each selected attachment is displayed in the input area with its filename or thumbnail
Users can remove an individual attachment without clearing the others
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Replace the attachment label with a paperclip icon
userStories.description: As a user, I want the attachment action to use a paperclip icon so that the UI looks polished and does not show the raw word attach.
acceptanceCriteria: The attachment trigger shows a paperclip icon
The visible attachment trigger does not display the text 'attach'
The attachment trigger remains accessible via an aria-label or equivalent accessible name
Clicking the paperclip icon opens the image file picker
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Send attached images to AI analysis
userStories.description: As a user, I want attached game UI screenshots to be included in the AI request so that the AI can analyze the UI design, layout, and image content.
acceptanceCriteria: Image attachments are preserved in the request payload sent to the AI analysis path
The AI prompt or request explicitly asks for game UI design analysis, layout analysis, and image-function analysis
Multiple attached images are included in a single analysis request
Reference images and images intended for inclusion can both be represented in the request payload
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Render preview in locked mobile phone ratio
userStories.description: As a user, I want the generated UI preview to appear inside a phone-shaped frame using the real 750 by 1624 design resolution so that the preview matches the target mobile layout.
acceptanceCriteria: The preview area renders inside a phone-style frame
The preview content uses a locked 750:1624 aspect ratio
The preview scales to fit the available screen without changing the underlying aspect ratio
The preview clearly communicates the target resolution as 750 × 1624 or equivalent
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Replace UI node preview with requirement breakdown tree
userStories.description: As a user, I want to preview the requirement breakdown as a tree so that I can understand the generated structure without seeing the old UI node preview.
acceptanceCriteria: The old UI node preview is removed from the preview interface
A requirement breakdown tree is shown in the preview interface
The tree supports parent and child requirement levels
Each tree item displays a concise requirement title or label
The tree has a deterministic empty state when no requirements are available
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Redesign the overall layout for attachments, phone preview, and requirement tree
userStories.description: As a user, I want the page layout adjusted around multi-image attachments, the phone preview, and the requirement tree so that the workflow remains clear and usable.
acceptanceCriteria: The page layout visibly separates input, attachments, phone preview, and requirement tree areas
The phone preview and requirement tree can be viewed at the same time on desktop-sized windows
The layout remains usable at narrower widths without horizontal page overflow
Attachment thumbnails or filenames do not overlap the prompt input, preview, or tree areas
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
